content.problemStatement:
You are given two arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN. You can perform the following types of operations on array AAA.


Type 111: Select any prefix of AAA and increment all its elements by 111.
Type 222: Select any suffix of AAA and increment all its elements by 111.
Your task is to transform the array AAA into array BBB using the **minimum** number of operations. If it is impossible to do so, output −1-1−1.

For an array AAA having NNN elements:


A **prefix** of the array AAA is a subarray starting at index 111.
A **suffix** of the array AAA is a subarray ending at index NNN.

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the size of arrays AAA and BBB.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes AiA_iAi​.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes BiB_iBi​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ―  **minimum** operations required to convert AAA into BBB. Print −1-1−1 if it is impossible to convert AAA into BBB.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Given A=[2,3,5,1,2]A = [2,3,5,1,2]A=[2,3,5,1,2]. It can be converted to array B=[4,3,6,2,3]B = [4, 3, 6, 2, 3]B=[4,3,6,2,3] in 333 operations:

Operation 111: Perform a type 111 operation. Choose the prefix of length 111 and increment the elements by 111. Thus, the updated array is A=[3,3,5,1,2]A = [3, 3, 5, 1, 2]A=[3,3,5,1,2].
Operation 222: Perform a type 111 operation. Choose the prefix of length 111 and increment the elements by 111. Thus, the updated array is A=[4,3,5,1,2]A = [4, 3, 5, 1, 2]A=[4,3,5,1,2].
Operation 333: Perform a type 222 operation. Choose the suffix of length 333 and increment the elements by 111. Thus, the updated array is A=[4,3,6,2,3]A = [4, 3, 6, 2, 3]A=[4,3,6,2,3].

It can be proven that we cannot convert the array AAA into array BBB in less than 333 operations.
Test Case 222: Given A=[0,0,0,0]A = [0,0,0,0]A=[0,0,0,0]. It can be converted to array B=[1,2,2,1]B = [1,2,2,1]B=[1,2,2,1] in 222 operations:

Operation 111: Perform a type 111 operation. Choose the prefix of length 333 and increment the elements by 111. Thus, the updated array is A=[1,1,1,0]A = [1,1,1,0]A=[1,1,1,0].
Operation 222: Perform a type 222 operation. Choose the suffix of length 333 and increment the elements by 111. Thus, the updated array is A=[1,2,2,1]A = [1,2,2,1]A=[1,2,2,1].

It can be proven that we cannot convert the array AAA into array BBB in less than 222 operations.
Test Case 333: Given A=[1,2,3]A = [1,2,3]A=[1,2,3]. The value of A[3]A[3]A[3] will always be greater than B[3]B[3]B[3]. Hence, it is impossible to convert AAA into BBB using any number of operations.
